messages.content: Since you have logged this conversation can we look back and see if any of the conversation applies to changing Agents.md/Rules/Workflow at all PResent me with your proposals
Based on our conversation analysis, here are my proposals for changes that should apply to Agents.md, Rules, and Workflow files across all agents...
Perfect! I'll implement all of these proposals to create a comprehensive standard for all agents...
Are we sure we are following best practices?
You're absolutely right to question this. Let me research current best practices for multi-agent AI systems and project structure...
Based on the research, it appears our current approach is NOT following industry best practices for multi-agent systems. Here are the key findings...
hold on run this same thing through the architect workflow
I'll run this through the Architect workflow properly. Let me follow the workflow steps...

Lets keep our IDE architecture simple like we have now. We can play with more advanced stuff with Sovereign AI Later as I think Creating an orchestrator at this level adds complexity? What are your thoughts do you think Orchestrator > Workers is our best approach, Its kind of Ironic as its exactly what we will build with Sovereign AI
You're absolutely right, and that's a brilliant observation that I completely missed. The irony you've identified is that we would essentially be building the same pattern twice...
Specification and agent template deleted. We're keeping our simple, effective approach that aligns with constitutional principles.

context.task: Decide on multi-agent architecture approach based on best practices research

summary: Decision made to keep simple IDE architecture, avoiding premature implementation of application-level patterns in infrastructure
